title: Encrypted wi-fi provisioning
abstract: A method for group provisioning for a number of Wi-Fi devices to enable them to connect to a wireless network. The method utilizes an identity-based broadcasting encryption (IBBE) like scheme in a SmartConfig scenario to facilitate an efficient group Wi-Fi provisioning and improve network security at the same time. It also discloses a configurator, as well as a W-Fi device, to implement the group provisioning method.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method of provisioning a number N of Wi-Fi devices with a configurator to enable the Wi-Fi devices to connect to a wireless network, wherein the configurator is assigned with a public key PK, and each of the Wi-Fi devices is assigned with a device identifier ID and a private device key skID calculated based on the device identifier ID and a master secret key MSK, and wherein the public key PK and the master secret key MSK are produced from a security parameter P and a maximum value of the number N, the method comprising: at the configurator: receiving a set S of the device ID for each one of the Wi-Fi receiver devices; generating a session key K and a header H based on the set S and the public key PK, wherein the session key K is embraced in the header H; encrypting network credentials for connection to the wireless network with the session key K; and broadcasting or multicasting messages that include the header H and the encrypted network credentials, and at any one of said Wi-Fi devices: listening in a promiscuous mode, and, upon receiving the messages from the configurator, abstracting the header H and the encrypted network credentials; decrypting the header H using the private device key skID to obtain the session key K; and retrieving the network credentials by decrypting the encrypted network credentials using the obtained session key K. 2. The method of claim 1, further comprising broadcasting or multicasting the set S from the configurator, and, at any one of said Wi-Fi devices, checking whether the device ID of this Wi-Fi device is present in the set S received, and, based on the device ID being present in the set S, performing the decrypting and retrieving steps. 3. The method of any one of claims 1 or 2, further comprising receiving, at the configurator, the network credentials from a user, wherein the network credentials comprise at least a network identifier SSID and a password. 4. The method of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein encrypting the network credentials is based on a symmetric encryption algorithm, such as an Advanced Encryption Standard AES cryptography algorithm. 5. The method of any one of claims 1 to 4, further comprising encoding the messages before the broadcasting or multicasting steps. 6. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein receiving the device ID for each one of the Wi-Fi devices is performed by scanning a unique QR code indicative of each one of the Wi-Fi devices. 7. A configurator for provisioning a number N of Wi-Fi devices to enable them to connect to a wireless network, wherein the configurator is assigned with a public key PK, and each of the Wi-Fi devices is assigned with a device identifier ID and a private device key skID calculated based on the device identifier ID and a master secret key MSK, and wherein the public key PK and the master secret key MSK are produced from a security parameter P and a maximum value of the number N, the configurator comprising: means for receiving a set S of the device ID for each one of the Wi-Fi devices; means for generating a session key K and a header H based on the set S and the public key PK, wherein the session key K is embraced in the header H; means for encrypting network credentials for connection to the wireless network with the session key K; and means for broadcasting or multicasting messages including the header H and the encrypted network credentials. 8. The configurator according to claim 7, wherein the means for broadcasting or multicasting broadcasts or multicasts the set S. 9. The configurator of claim 7 or 8, further comprising means for receiving the network credentials from a user, wherein the network credentials comprise at least a network identifier SSID and a password. 10. The configurator of any one of claims 7 to 9, wherein the means for encrypting the network credentials encrypts the network credentials based on a symmetric encryption algorithm, such as the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) cryptography algorithm. 11. The configurator of any one of claims 7 to 10, further comprising means for encoding the messages before broadcasting or multicasting. 12. The configurator of any one of claims 7 to 11, further comprising means for scanning a unique QR code indicative of each of the Wi-Fi devices to obtain the device IDs. 13. A Wi-Fi device having a device identifier ID and capable of being provisioned by a configurator to connect to a wireless network with network credentials, wherein the configurator is assigned with a public key PK, and the Wi-Fi devices is assigned with a private device key skID that is calculated based on the device identifier ID and a master secret key MSK, and wherein the public key PK and the master secret key MSK are produced from a security parameter P and a maximum value of the number N of Wi-Fi devices that the configurator is capable of provisioning, the Wi-Fi device comprising: means for listening in a promiscuous mode and receiving messages from the configurator; means for abstracting a header H and encrypted network credentials from the messages received from the configurator; means for decrypting the header H to obtain a session key K, using the private device key skID; and means for retrieving the network credentials by decrypting the encrypted network credentials using the session key K. 14. The Wi-Fi device of claim 13, further comprising means for connecting to the wireless network using the decrypted network credentials.
